weeks.weekly_summary: Establish aerobic foundation with 2 easy runs. Low volume week builds into the block. Focus on consistent effort and form.
weeks.coaching_notes: Recent 14-day average shows ~5 km per run. Starting conservatively at 2 runs/week (Monday, Wednesday) to fit 4-hour budget. Athlete is ramping up from light volume (9.9 km in 2 weeks). This week establishes the schedule rhythm. All runs easy Zone 2. Quality session (1 tempo interval block) will be introduced Week 2 as fitness builds.
recovery_flags: Low recent volume — monitor RPE to ensure no overtraining as volume increases
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 2 introduces first quality session (tempo intervals). Volume increases to ~26 km. Wednesday becomes the quality session day.

days.title: Rest
days.description: Full recovery day. Prepare for Week 1 start Monday.

days.title: Easy 10 km
days.description: Conversational pace easy run. Focus on relaxed cadence and steady breathing. No pressure on effort — this is pure aerobic base.
days.notes: Start of 4-week block. Maintain ~6:30 min/km pace (slightly slower than recent 5:50–6:10 average to keep Zone 2).

days.title: Pilates Session
days.description: Mobility, core stability, and flexibility work. Complements running with functional strength.
days.notes: Light activity — safe to place adjacent to easy runs.

days.title: Easy 10 km
days.description: Second easy run of the week. Same effort as Monday — steady, conversational pace. Build consistency.
days.notes: Maintain steady Zone 2 effort. No speed focus this week.

days.title: Rest
days.description: Recovery between runs. Hydrate and fuel well.

days.title: Gym Session
days.description: Strength and conditioning. Focus on lower body, glutes, and core stability for running power and injury prevention.
days.notes: Moderate intensity. Schedule allows 1 day buffer before football Saturday.

days.title: Football Match
days.description: Recreational football. Provides lateral movement and agility work complementary to linear running.
days.notes: High-intensity cross-training. Counts as part of overall weekly training stimulus.

weeks.weekly_summary: Introduce first quality session (tempo intervals). Build aerobic endurance while keeping volume manageable. 2 runs/week, strategic placement.
weeks.coaching_notes: Volume increases to 26 km (4 hours running). Wednesday becomes the quality session day (tempo run). Monday remains easy recovery run. Tempo intervals at ~4:45–4:50 min/km (slightly faster than HM goal pace of 5:55 min/km, which builds threshold fitness). 1 quality session per week fits polarized model. Athlete should feel strong HR and perceived effort rise during tempo blocks.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 3 increases volume further (~31 km). Tempo work becomes more sustained. Consider adding a longer easy run (12–13 km) and maintaining 1 quality session.

days.title: Rest
days.description: Recovery day. Reflect on Week 1 execution and prepare for Week 2.

days.title: Easy 10 km
days.description: Easy recovery run. Keep pace conversational and relaxed. Recover from weekend football and prepare for Wednesday tempo.
days.notes: Same effort as Week 1 Monday/Wednesday. Zone 2 only.

days.title: Pilates Session
days.description: Flexibility, core, and mobility work. Prepares body for Wednesday tempo session.
days.notes: Light activity — day before quality workout is acceptable.

days.title: Tempo Intervals: 2×8 min @ Tempo
days.description: Warm-up 2 km easy (12 min). Then 2 repetitions of 8 minutes at tempo pace (4:45–4:50 min/km, ~170–175 bpm for typical athlete). 2 min easy recovery jog between reps. Cool-down 2 km easy (12 min). This builds lactate threshold and prepares aerobic system for half-marathon pace work.
days.notes: Quality session of the week. Tempo pace is ~10–15 sec/km faster than HM goal pace (5:55). HR should be elevated but sustainable. First quality workout — athlete should feel the difference in effort.

days.title: Rest
days.description: Full recovery after quality session. Hydrate and refuel well.

days.title: Gym Session
days.description: Strength and conditioning. Lower body, glutes, core. Avoid heavy leg work that conflicts with quality running.
days.notes: Moderate intensity. 1 day before football.

days.title: Football Match
days.description: Recreational football. Movement, agility, and power work.
days.notes: Counts as part of overall training stimulus. 2 days after quality session allows adequate recovery.

weeks.weekly_summary: Increase volume and extend tempo duration. Longer easy run introduced (12 km). 1 quality session per week (extended tempo). Building toward peak phase.
weeks.coaching_notes: Volume reaches 31 km (4+ hours). Phase shifts to Aerobic Development (now 11 weeks to race). Monday is longer easy run (12 km). Wednesday is quality session — tempo extends to 3×6 min to build sustained threshold capacity. This builds the aerobic engine and prepares for faster sustained paces in Weeks 13–15. Athlete should feel stronger in tempo blocks. Monitor fatigue — if any signs of overtraining, reduce tempo volume next week.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 4 is final week of this block. Tempo work increases slightly (3×7 min or 1×15 min sustained). Prepare for transition to next 4-week block focused on longer sustained efforts and race-pace work.

days.title: Rest
days.description: Recovery and preparation for Week 3.

days.title: Easy 12 km
days.description: Longer easy run to build aerobic endurance. Maintain conversational pace throughout (6:30 min/km or slightly slower). This is a 'long run' for the week but at easy effort — critical for aerobic base in Aerobic Development phase.
days.notes: Longest run yet. Keep pace easy — the distance itself builds adaptation. No speed target.

days.title: Pilates Session
days.description: Mobility, core, flexibility. Support recovery after Monday long run.
days.notes: Light activity — active recovery.

days.title: Tempo Intervals: 3×6 min @ Tempo
days.description: Warm-up 2 km easy (12 min). Then 3 repetitions of 6 minutes at tempo pace (4:45–4:50 min/km). 2 min easy recovery between reps. Cool-down 2 km easy (12 min). Builds sustained threshold capacity and prepares aerobic system for race-pace work.
days.notes: Second week of quality sessions. Volume increases (3 reps vs 2 last week) while keeping individual rep duration sustainable. Focus on consistent effort across all 3 reps.

days.title: Rest
days.description: Recovery after Wednesday quality session.

days.title: Gym Session
days.description: Strength and conditioning. Lower body, glutes, core stability.
days.notes: Moderate intensity. Support running fitness with functional strength.

days.title: Football Match
days.description: Recreational football. Agility, lateral movement, power.
days.notes: 3 days after Wednesday quality session — adequate recovery.

weeks.weekly_summary: Final week of block. Extend tempo work to sustained duration. Prepare for transition to next block (Peak Training, Weeks 5–8). Build confidence in tempo/threshold fitness.
weeks.coaching_notes: Volume peaks at 32 km. Phase remains Aerobic Development (10 weeks to race). Monday is 12 km easy run (same as Week 3). Wednesday is quality session — tempo extends to 1×12 min sustained (or 4×5 min if athlete prefers intervals). This builds race-pace capacity and prepares for peak training phase (Weeks 5–8 in next block). After this block, expect to introduce longer sustained efforts and race-pace segments. Monitor fatigue and adjust intensity if needed for next block.
weeks.next_week_preview: Next 4-week block (Weeks 5–8, 10–11 weeks to race) shifts to Peak Training. Introduce longer runs with race-pace segments (21 km long runs with 8–10 km at HM pace). Tempo remains but becomes secondary to race-specific work. Volume may increase further or stabilize depending on athlete adaptation.

days.title: Rest
days.description: Recovery day. Reflect on 4-week block performance.

days.title: Easy 12 km
days.description: Final long easy run of the block. Maintain conversational pace (6:30 min/km or slightly slower). Build aerobic endurance and confidence in sustaining effort.
days.notes: Same as Week 3 Monday. No speed target — pure distance and aerobic adaptation.

days.title: Pilates Session
days.description: Mobility and core work. Support recovery and prepare for Wednesday quality session.
days.notes: Light activity — active recovery.

days.title: Sustained Tempo: 1×12 min @ Tempo
days.description: Warm-up 2 km easy (12 min). Then 1 sustained effort of 12 minutes at tempo pace (4:45–4:50 min/km). Cool-down 2 km easy (12 min). Builds aerobic capacity and teaches the body to sustain threshold effort. This mimics the 'homestretch' of the half-marathon race.
days.notes: Final quality session of the block. Extended duration (12 min) requires mental toughness and aerobic fitness. If athlete struggles, reduce to 4×5 min intervals with 2 min recovery. Should feel strong and ready to progress to race-pace work in next block.

days.title: Rest
days.description: Recovery after final quality session of the block.

days.title: Gym Session
days.description: Strength and conditioning. Final session of the block. Lower body, glutes, core.
days.notes: Moderate intensity. Prepare body for transition to next block.

days.title: Football Match
days.description: Recreational football. Final cross-training session of the block.
days.notes: 3 days after Wednesday quality session — adequate recovery. Closing activity for the 4-week Base/Aerobic Development block.

coaching_overview:
### 4-Week Base Building & Aerobic Development Block

**Block Goals:**
- Establish aerobic foundation and build running habit (2 runs/week, 4-hour weekly budget)
- Introduce polarized training with 1 quality session per week (tempo intervals)
- Progress from 20 km (Week 1) to 32 km (Week 4) while maintaining 75–85% easy effort
- Prepare fitness and resilience for Peak Training phase (Weeks 5–8, introducing race-pace work)

**Phase Progression:**
- **Weeks 1–2: Base Building** (15–14 weeks to race) — Establish schedule, 2 easy runs + 1 tempo quality session
- **Weeks 3–4: Aerobic Development** (11–10 weeks to race) — Extend easy runs to 12 km, build tempo capacity (1×12 min sustained)

**Weekly Structure (Mon/Wed Runs):**
- **Monday:** Easy run (10–12 km) — aerobic endurance foundation
- **Wednesday:** Quality session (tempo intervals) — lactate threshold development
- **Tue, Fri, Sat:** Cross-training (Pilates, Gym, Football) — complementary work and injury prevention

**Total Block Distance:** 109 km running + cross-training stimulus

**Key Adaptations:**
- Recent volume is low (~10 km in 14 days), so the plan ramps conservatively: Week 1 = 20 km, Week 4 = 32 km (50% increase over 4 weeks, safe progression)
- Quality sessions increase progressively: Week 1/2 = 2×8 min → Week 3 = 3×6 min → Week 4 = 1×12 min sustained
- Tempo pace (4:45–4:50 min/km) is 10–15 sec/km faster than half-marathon goal pace (5:55), building threshold fitness
- All easy runs maintain Zone 2 effort (6:30 min/km baseline) to preserve aerobic base and enforce 80/20 polarization

**Next Steps (Week 5+):**
- Transition to Peak Training phase (Weeks 5–8)
- Introduce longer runs (18–21 km) with sustained race-pace segments (8–10 km at ~5:55 min/km)
- Quality sessions evolve to race-pace intervals (e.g., 4×3 km @ HM pace) or fartlek
- Increase volume gradually toward 40–45 km/week peak
- Monitor fatigue markers (HRV, RPE, sleep) — adjust intensity if needed

**Race Preparation (Weeks 9–16):**
- Weeks 9–12: Peak intensity and volume; race-simulation long runs
- Weeks 13–15: Taper (reduce volume 20–30% each week, maintain intensity)
- Week 16: Race week — minimal running, final preparations

**Athlete Notes:**
- Consistent Monday/Wednesday schedule fits 4-hour budget and allows adequate recovery
- Cross-training (Pilates, Gym, Football) provides strength, mobility, and mental variety without conflicting with running
- Aim for sub-2:00 half-marathon (5:55 min/km pace) — this plan builds the aerobic and threshold fitness needed
- Track daily RPE, HRV (if available), and sleep; adjust if fatigue accumulates
